title: IMGUI Incremental Update
use_for: designing retained update pipelines for IMGUI frameworks
planning reusable presentation resources with explicit invalidation
building binding-aware partial updates for windows, panels, and component subtrees
do_not_use_for: visual styling questions without update-model concerns
architectures that accept whole-tree invalidation as the default
rules: generate expensive presentation resources once and reuse them until explicit invalidation
compare source data cheaply before recomputing derived presentation
mark only the smallest affected host, panel, or subtree dirty
stop recomputation at the first clean boundary
procedure: List the expensive derived resources and bound presentation data.
Assign ownership and invalidation rules for each resource.
Define binding checks or version stamps for source changes.
Introduce dirty propagation through explicit subtree boundaries.
Verify that clean siblings can skip recomputation safely.

primary_when: the request is specifically about bindings, dirty flags, partial refresh, resource reuse, or subtree-level updates in an IMGUI framework
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about overall host architecture, input routing, or basic layout composition

solves: how to avoid recomputing more presentation than the state change requires
how to combine resource reuse, binding checks, and dirty propagation coherently

core_principles: Derived presentation should be retained separately from domain truth.
Binding checks should be cheaper than the recomputation they guard.
Dirty propagation should stop at meaningful subtree boundaries.
constraints: Binding state must not become authoritative application state.
Incremental update paths must remain understandable during debugging and review.
decision_rules: Use version stamps or similarly cheap source signals before considering deeper comparisons.
Invalidate at panel or component subtree scope before escalating to whole-window scope.
Treat global restyles or structural changes as legitimate broad invalidation events.
Attach data-oriented-pipeline support when the UI update model should be designed as an explicit stage pipeline rather than only as widget-local invalidation logic.

preferred_patterns: host or panel-owned derived presentation caches
explicit dirty boundaries at host, panel, and component subtree levels
incremental UI updates expressed as explicit pipeline stages when architectural scale justifies it
avoid_patterns: deep per-frame equality checks over large source graphs
ad hoc per-widget cache ownership with unclear cleanup
treating the update model as a collection of widget-local tricks instead of one owned dataflow path

recipes.name: Refresh only one bound panel subtree
recipes.when: Use when one panel depends on data that changed but sibling panels do not.
steps: Detect the change through a source version or explicit dirty notification.
Mark the owning panel subtree dirty.
Recompute only the derived resources used by that subtree.
Leave clean siblings and their presentation caches untouched.

pitfalls: Making everything dirty so often that the incremental model gives no benefit.
Using binding comparisons that cost more than recomputation.
Allowing stale derived resources because invalidation ownership is unclear.
review_checklist: Can updates stop at a subtree boundary?
Are binding checks cheaper than the work they skip?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Incremental Update Model
references.interpretation: Use this first for retained resource reuse, binding-aware updates, and subtree-level dirty propagation.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for optimized incremental UI updates in the domain.

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI State Patterns
references.interpretation: Use this to align binding and dirty-state ownership with the broader retained-state model.
references.why: Use to ground incremental updates in explicit state ownership.

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Performance Guidelines
references.interpretation: Use this to evaluate whether the incremental model actually reduces cost instead of shifting it.
references.why: Use to validate the performance tradeoffs of bindings, caches, and partial refresh.

references.title: Local Reference: Pipeline Architecture
references.interpretation: Use this when the UI update path should be elevated from local invalidation rules to an explicit data-oriented pipeline architecture.
references.why: Use to treat UI update flow as a pipeline-first architectural path where scale and performance justify it.

references.title: Local Reference: Invalidation Propagation
references.interpretation: Use this to formalize how local UI state changes should propagate through a broader pipeline of derived presentation products.
references.why: Use to connect UI dirty propagation with explicit stage-level invalidation rules.

references.title: Local Reference: Execution Model
references.interpretation: Use this when UI updates must be scheduled as a predictable execution graph rather than an informal redraw cascade.
references.why: Use to connect incremental UI refresh with explicit pipeline execution planning.
